text.title: Gcp Llm Prompts Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gcp-llm-prompts methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Cost-review tasks MUST use the curated cost-review prompt template; ad-hoc prompts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: The curated template enforces a consistent JSON output shape that downstream automation can rely on.

rule.statement: IAM-audit tasks MUST use the curated IAM-audit prompt template.
rule.rationale: Audit findings need consistent severity/principal/resource fields for ticketing automation.

rule.statement: Network-review tasks MUST use the curated network-review prompt template.
rule.rationale: Network reviews need a consistent topology + control-plane diff format for downstream IaC generation.

rule.statement: Every prompt MUST require JSON output conforming to a declared schema; free-text output is forbidden for automation-bound prompts.
rule.rationale: Free text breaks downstream tooling; JSON Schema + validator is the only sustainable contract.

rule.statement: Curated templates MUST carry a `template_id` + `version`; consumers MUST pin to a specific version.
rule.rationale: Unversioned templates create silent behaviour drift between releases.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
